profile.name: Cường Q. Huỳnh
profile.title: Master Orchestrator · IP Assets Professional · Data Architect
profile.summary: Building intelligent systems at the intersection of knowledge governance, infrastructure architecture, and multi-agent AI orchestration.
profile.about: I operate at the convergence of information science, technical architecture, and AI orchestration. My work spans custodianship systems for intellectual property, multi-agent workflows, and governance models that ensure traceability, integrity, and long-term institutional memory. Every artifact carries a clear chain of custody — from concept to Canon.

bullets: Designed and deployed multi-agent AI orchestration systems coordinating Claude, GPT, Perplexity, and Gemini
Authored DESIGN-CANON-2026-001: a ratified personal design system with cryptographic provenance
Built document custody framework using SHA-256 + UUIDv7 signing protocol
Established Virtual Library architecture on NotebookLM for institutional knowledge curation
Designed scalable data governance frameworks for enterprise knowledge assets
Architected end-to-end data pipelines with full traceability and audit trails
Led technical architecture reviews and strategic roadmap development

projects.title: Master Canon Design Specification
projects.summary: Personal universal design system synthesized from five source specifications. Four canonical themes, five morphism styles, full CSS variable system.

star.situation: Five scattered design specs existed independently with no unifying Canon.
star.task: Synthesise into a single authoritative design language covering all media.
star.action: Analysed conflicts (font stacks, morphism styles, color laws), resolved tensions, authored 10-part specification with cryptographic signing protocol.
star.result: Ratified DESIGN-CANON-2026-001 v1.0.0 — governing all visual output.

projects.title: Multi-Agent Knowledge Pipeline
projects.summary: Structured multi-agent workflow coordinating Claude, GPT, Perplexity, and Gemini for research synthesis with full traceability chains.

star.situation: Research synthesis required coordinating multiple AI systems without losing attribution.
star.task: Build an orchestration pipeline with full traceability and conflict resolution.
star.action: Designed agent role specifications, implemented cross-model routing, built attribution chain logging.
star.result: Fully attributed multi-source research outputs with zero untraced claims.

projects.title: Document Custody & Signing System
projects.summary: Provenance framework using SHA-256 hash-based signing, UUIDv7 timestamping, and multi-party signature chains for institutional documents.

star.situation: Institutional documents lacked verifiable provenance and non-repudiation.
star.task: Design a signing protocol that is both cryptographically sound and operationally simple.
star.action: Implemented composite signature: SHA-256(doc_hash|uuid_v7|timestamp|context). Built multi-layer sign chain: Contributor → System → Authority.
star.result: All project artifacts now carry verifiable, tamper-evident provenance records.

projects.title: NotebookLM Knowledge Architecture
projects.summary: Curated knowledge graph and research memory system enabling structured retrieval, cross-domain synthesis, and AI-assisted literature review.

star.situation: Research materials were fragmented across tools with no unified retrieval layer.
star.task: Build a persistent, curated knowledge architecture with AI-assisted synthesis.
star.action: Structured source taxonomy, built cross-domain link graph, implemented RAG retrieval layer on NotebookLM.
star.result: Reduced research retrieval time by ~70%; enabled cross-domain synthesis previously requiring hours of manual work.

posts.title: Why Every Document Needs a Signature Chain
posts.summary: Exploring cryptographic provenance for institutional knowledge assets and why SHA-256 + UUIDv7 is the right foundation.

posts.title: The 60/30/10 Color Law: Why It Works
posts.summary: A deep dive into why the 60/30/10 color distribution law produces consistently professional results across all media.

posts.title: Multi-Agent Systems: Roles, Routing, and Traceability
posts.summary: How to design agent role specifications that eliminate attribution loss in multi-model research pipelines.
